function.name: query_data
function.description: Search and filter rows from a dataset. Use this to answer questions about the data, find specific records, or look up IDs before updating or deleting.

filters.description: Column-value pairs to filter by. Supports comparison operators as string prefixes: '>500', '<=10', '!=active'. Example: {"City": "Seattle", "List Price": ">500000"}

limit.description: Maximum number of rows to return. Defaults to 20.

function.name: get_summary
function.description: Return aggregate statistics for a dataset. Use this for analytical questions. CRITICAL: If you do NOT provide 'group_by', this tool automatically returns ALL metrics (min, max, mean, median, count) for ALL numeric columns at once! You only need to call it ONCE to get ranges for Bedrooms, Bathrooms, Prices, etc. DO NOT call this tool multiple times in parallel.

filters.description: Optional column-value pairs to filter the dataset BEFORE summarizing. Example: {"City": "Chicago"}

group_by.description: Optional column name to group results by. Example: 'Channel' or 'City'.

metric.description: The aggregation metric. ONLY APPLIES IF 'group_by' IS PROVIDED. If 'group_by' is empty, this parameter is ignored and ALL metrics are returned automatically.

function.name: insert_row
function.description: Insert a new row into a dataset. IMPORTANT: Only call this after collecting ALL required fields from the user. Required fields for real_estate: Property Type, City, State, List Price. Required fields for marketing: Campaign Name, Channel, Budget Allocated.

row.description: A dict of column-value pairs for the new record. The ID will be auto-generated if not provided.

function.name: update_rows
function.description: Update fields in a row that matches the given filters. SAFETY: If the filter matches more than 1 row, the tool will return an Ambiguity Error — do NOT retry, ask the user to be more specific. Use query_data first if you are unsure how many rows match.

filters.description: Column-value pairs to identify the exact row to update. Use the record's ID for precision. Example: {"Campaign ID": "CMP-5"}

updates.description: Column-value pairs representing the new values to apply. Example: {"Budget Allocated": 20000}

function.name: delete_rows
function.description: Delete a row that matches the given filters. SAFETY: Only call this after user confirmation. If the filter matches more than 1 row, the tool returns an Ambiguity Error — do NOT retry, ask the user to clarify. Use query_data first if you are unsure of the exact record.

filters.description: Column-value pairs to identify the exact row to delete. Use the record's ID for precision. Example: {"Listing ID": "LST-12"}
